src/flit/platform/desktop/multi_window

  • Index

  • Vars

        allWindows
      • allWindows(): seq[FlitWindow]
        closeWindow
      • closeWindow(w: FlitWindow)
        defaultWindowConfig
      • defaultWindowConfig(): WindowConfig
        findWindow
      • findWindow(id: uint32): FlitWindow
        openWindow
      • openWindow(rootWidget: Widget; config: WindowConfig = defaultWindowConfig(); onClose: proc () = nil): FlitWindow
        windowCount
      • windowCount(): int

Multi-window support. openWindow(widget, config) adds another SDL window to the app; each gets its own canvas, binding, and event loop. Closing a secondary window leaves the app running; closing all windows quits.

The current implementation is single-threaded: every window's frame loop runs in the same runDesktop main loop, with events dispatched by SDL's window ID. The fast paths (layout cache, glyph atlas) are per-window, so opening a second window does not slow the first one's perf.

This module exposes the window registry; the desktop runner iterates windows in its event/paint loop.

Imports

../../foundation/widget, ../../foundation/render_object, ../../foundation/binding, ../../foundation/geometry, ../../foundation/runtime, ../../rendering/canvas_sdl

Types

FlitWindow = ref object
  id*: uint32
  window*: WindowPtr
  renderer*: RendererPtr
  canvas*: SdlCanvas
  binding*: Binding
  rootElement*: Element
  surfaceSize*: Size
  title*: string
  onClose*: proc () {.closure.}
A flit-managed SDL window. The desktop runner iterates a table of these and ticks each one per frame. 
WindowConfig = object
  title*: string
  width*, height*: int
  resizable*, highDpi*, vsync*: bool
  fontPath*: string
Construction settings for a new window. Pass to openWindow or take defaultWindowConfig() and tweak the fields you care about.

Vars

windows {.threadvar.}: Table[uint32, FlitWindow]
Registry of every open window keyed by SDL window ID. The desktop runner reads this each frame to dispatch events and paint. Exposed so callers can inspect window state directly; mutate via openWindow / closeWindow.

Procs

proc allWindows(): seq[FlitWindow] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns every registered window. Used by the runner to tick every window's frame loop. 
proc closeWindow(w: FlitWindow) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                  forbids: [].}
Closes a window and releases its SDL resources. The runner stops ticking it. If this was the last window, the app quits. 
proc defaultWindowConfig(): WindowConfig {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the standard window configuration: title "flit", 800x600, resizable, high-DPI, vsync on. Use as the base for per-window overrides. 
proc findWindow(id: uint32): FlitWindow {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Looks up a window by SDL window ID. Used by the runner to dispatch events to the right window. 
proc openWindow(rootWidget: Widget;
                config: WindowConfig = defaultWindowConfig();
                onClose: proc () = nil): FlitWindow {.
    ...raises: [PixieError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, RootEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}

Opens a new SDL window and mounts rootWidget into it. The returned FlitWindow is registered so the desktop runner picks it up on the next frame.

Call from a button handler (after runApp has started the main window) to open settings dialogs, secondary editors, or floating tool palettes.

proc windowCount(): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
How many windows are currently open. Reaches zero -> app quits.
